card.intuition: JavaScript's way of sharing methods and properties between objects, where every object has a 'link' to another object (its prototype) and searches up the chain until it finds the property.
card.analogy: A family tree of skills: you (the Object) know how to 'Play Guitar'. If someone asks you to 'Fix a Pipe' and you don't know how, you ask your Parent (Prototype). If they don't know, they ask your Grandparent, until someone knows or you reach the 'Original Ancestor' (Object.prototype).
card.time_complexity: O(D) where D is depth of the chain
when_to_use: Creating memory-efficient libraries (sharing one method across 1000s of objects)
Understanding how 'Classes' in JS work (they are just sugar for prototypes)
gotchas: Modifying `Object.prototype` directly is dangerous as it affects EVERY object in your entire application
The `__proto__` property is the link to the parent, while the `.prototype` property on a function is what it uses for NEW objects it creates

exercise.prompt: Identify the top-most object in the prototype chain that is the 'parent' of almost all JS objects.
exercise.starter_code:
def find_root():
    # In JS: array.__proto__.__proto__ === _______.prototype
    root = "Object"
    return root
hints: Everything in JS is basically an ______
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "object"
assert check("Object")
print("PASS")
